items.hook: American Civil War: The CSS Arkansas, the most effective ironclad on the Mississippi River, battles with Union Navy ships commanded by Admiral David Farragut, severely damaging three ships and sustaining heavy damage herself.
items.body: The American Civil War was a civil war in the United States between the Union and the Confederacy, which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: The Art of Opposition by Courttia Newland review – piercing essays on culture and creativity
items.body:
The novelist issues a inspiring call for artists to exercise their autonomy in a world of gatekeepers

In 1988, the late Ghanaian writer and filmmaker Kwesi Owusu edited Storms of the Heart: An Anthology of Black Arts & Culture,

items.hook: A classicist’s verdict on Nolan’s Odyssey: a soulful hero flatters our times as women and nuance pushed overboard
items.body: Matt Damon’s sensitive and repentant Odysseus might come as a surprise to Homer, likewise some significant omissions concerning the poem’s female characters

items.hook: The Gadget Maker is a 1954 novel by Maxwell Griffith.
items.body: It is notable for its vivid depiction of an otherwise-rarely-described milieu: campus life at MIT in the 1940s. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Black Beauty is a 1933 American pre-Code drama film directed by Phil Rosen and starring Esther Ralston, Alexander Kirkland and Gavin Gordon.
items.body: It is one of a number of adaptations of Anna Sewell's 1877 novel Black Beauty, with the setting moved from Victorian Britain to a plantation in Virginia. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Room With No Doors is an original novel written by Kate Orman and based on the long-running British science fiction television series Doctor Who.
items.body: It features the Seventh Doctor and Chris. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Luha ng Babae is a 1913 Tagalog-language novel written by Filipino novelist Mamerto A.
items.body: The 121-page book was published by the Limbagang Magiting Ni Honorio Lopez in Manila, Philippines during the American period in Philippine history. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Odyssey review – Nolan goes god-tier with breathtaking epic of men, monsters and moral metamorphosis
items.body: Doing full justice to the Homeric legend, Christopher Nolan amasses an epic cast to convey the true cost of war with film-making of thrilling ambition

items.hook: First Crusade: Christian soldiers take the Church of the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem after the final assault of a difficult siege.
items.body: The First Crusade (1096–1099) was the first of a series of religious wars, or Crusades, which were initiated, supported and at times directed by the Latin Church in the Middle Ages. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Shakira review – she-wolf roars again in playful victory lap from Colombian superstar
items.body:

Ahead of her World Cup final performance, the singer’s Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran tour whips through exuberant hits

items.hook: Dedication of the Temple of Castor and Pollux in ancient Rome.
items.body: The Temple of Castor and Pollux was an ancient temple in the Roman Forum, Rome, Central Italy. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Blessings is a 2003 American made-for-television drama film directed by Arvin Brown.
items.body: Based on the 2002 novel of the same name by Anna Quindlen, the film stars Mary Tyler Moore and Liam Waite. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The species is native to East Africa, and introduced to Hawaii, Florida, and California.
items.body: Jackson's chameleon, also known commonly as Jackson's horned chameleon, the three-horned chameleon, and the Kikuyu three-horned chameleon, is a species of chameleon, a lizard in the family Chamaeleonidae. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: A Belgian Air Force C-130 Hercules carrying the Royal Netherlands Army marching band crashes on landing at Eindhoven Airport.
items.body: The Belgian Air Force is the air arm of the Belgian Armed Forces. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: In his book Clinical Psychiatry, Emil Kraepelin gives a name to Alzheimer's disease, naming it after his colleague Alois Alzheimer.
items.body: Emil Wilhelm Georg Magnus Kraepelin was a German psychiatrist. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Unheard David Bowie songs from 1965 to be released – including ones with Jimmy Page on guitar
items.body:
Compilation entitled David Bowie: The Shel Talmy Recordings will be released in September, collating material from when he recorded as Davy Jones

Unheard 1965 recordings by David Bowie, from when he was starting out in swinging 60s

items.hook: Caspian Airlines Flight 7908 crashes near Jannatabad, Qazvin, Iran, killing 168.
items.body: Caspian Airlines Flight 7908 was a scheduled commercial flight from Tehran, Iran, to Yerevan, Armenia, that crashed near the village of Jannatabad, outside the city of Qazvin in north-western Iran, on 15 July 2009. One of those things you can't un-know.
